microphones.name: U47 Clone
microphones.location: Computer closet (not enough space in mic locker)
microphones.notes: Large diaphragm tube-style condenser. Handle with care.

preamps.name: 1073 Clone
preamps.notes: Classic warm, thick character. Channels 1-2 on the sidecar. Snake channels 1-2.

preamps.name: 1073 Clone
preamps.notes: Channels 3-4 on the sidecar. Snake channels 3-4.

preamps.name: API 312 Clone
preamps.notes: Punchy, forward midrange. Great on drums and guitars. Snake channels 5-6.

preamps.name: AEA Ribbon Mic Preamp
preamps.notes: Currently out for repair. Designed specifically for ribbon mics.

preamps.name: Sabatron Tube Preamp
preamps.notes: Beautiful on vocals. Has 30dB of built-in pad. Left off by default due to barely audible hum.

preamps.name: Psytech
preamps.notes: Connected via wall panel in live room (not through the snake). Very clean, transformer-less design. Not meant for saturation.

preamps.name: FMR Preamp
preamps.notes: Used for the talkback mic. Phantom power must be enabled for talkback to work.

converters.name: Lynx Aurora 16
converters.notes: Connected via AES. Additional conversion capacity.

outboard.name: Little Labs Fader Bank
outboard.notes: Inline after channels 1-8, between preamp outputs and A/D inputs. Use when driving preamps hard to prevent clipping the converters. 3dB steps.

outboard.name: Compressor Channel Strip
outboard.notes: Has two preamps built in. Rarely used.

monitoring.name: Genelec Monitors (pair)
monitoring.notes: Power buttons are behind the mounting brackets — tricky to reach. Same spot on both sides (not mirrored). Always turn on LAST, off FIRST.

monitoring.name: Presonus HP-60
monitoring.notes: Always plugged in. 4 headphone outputs, each with volume control. Has blend knob between Cue Mix A and Cue Mix B.

monitoring.name: MYO Console
monitoring.notes: Latency-free monitoring with 64-bit summing. Split monitoring: signal goes to DAW and monitors simultaneously. Has templates for common setups.

monitoring.name: Euphonics Controller
monitoring.notes: Soft power button is tucked behind the mounting bracket. Does not power on with the main power conditioner — needs individual power-on.

synths.name: Prophet
synths.notes: Line-level output, loud enough to bypass preamp and go straight to A/D.

synths.name: Yamaha SY-77
synths.notes: Emits high-frequency noise when powered on. Left off by default — only turn on when needed.

synths.name: Oberheim Matrix 1000
synths.notes: No keyboard — must be played via MIDI from another synth or controller. Mechanical noise when on. Left off by default.

synths.name: Oberheim Matrix 1000
synths.notes: Second unit. No keyboard — must be played via MIDI. Mechanical noise when on. Left off by default.

other.name: Cloud Lifters
other.notes: Clean gain boost for dynamic and ribbon mics.

other.name: DI Boxes
other.notes: For connecting guitars, bass, keyboards to preamp inputs.

other.name: Guitar Pedals
other.notes: Various guitar effects pedals available for sessions.

other.name: Expression Pedals
other.notes: For use with synths and effects.

other.name: Extra Headphone Amps
other.notes: Additional headphone amps if the HP-60 isn't enough outputs.

other.name: Leslie Speaker
other.notes: Working Leslie on wheels. Drag it into position for organ or guitar. Great for that classic rotating speaker sound.
